Q1: The system adheres strictly to standard international news categories without introducing major country-specific sub-categories.
Q2: The system must absolutely handle code-switching as a core requirement.
Q3: The system would prioritize the flexible register accepted by professional journalists and editors over strict adherence to official orthographic guidelines. However, this would indeed naturally create borderline cases where a formal authority might disagree with an editorial choice.
Q4: The system generates individual significance scores for each output, which are visible to editors. If any score crosses a pre-defined threshold, the article is flagged, allowing the team to see exactly which criterion triggered the review. While the system does not provide human-readable explanations by default, editors can request them if recurring issues arise or if specific cases require deeper debugging.
